title: Sonnet--The Love Of Narcissus
content:
Like him who met his own eyes in the river,
         The poet trembles at his own long gaze
         That meets him through the changing nights

title: The Modern Poet - A Song Of Derivations
content:

title: Renouncement
content:

title: Via, Et Veritas, Et Vita
content:
"You never attained to Him?" "If to attain
        Be to abide, then that may be."
    "Endless the way, followed with how much pain!"

title: Unto Us A Son Is Given
content:
Given, not lent,
        And not withdrawn--once sent--
    This Infant of mankind, this One,
    Is still the little welcome Son.

title: Regrets
content:

title: To A Lost Melody
content:

content:

title: To A Poet
content:

content:

title: Sonnet

title: Sonnet--The Poet To Nature
content:
I have no secrets from thee, lyre sublime,
         My lyre whereof I make my melody.

title: Sonnet--The Neophyte
content:

         This yet unfaded and a faded brow;

title: The Fold
content:
Behold,
    The time is now! Bring back, bring back
    Thy flocks of fancies, wild of whim.
    Oh lead them from the mountain-track--

title: Why Wilt Thou Chide?
content:
Why wilt thou chide,
    Who hast attained to be denied?
        Oh learn, above
    All price is my refusal, Love.

title: Veneration Of Images
content:

title: In Autumn
content:
The leaves are many under my feet,
         And drift one way.

title: A Dead Harvest [In Kensington Gardens]
content:
Along the graceless grass of town

    Dead leaves, unlike the rows of hay,

title: Sonnet--Thoughts In Separation
content:
We never meet; yet we meet day by day
         Upon those hills of life, dim and immense:
         The good we love, and sleep--our innocence.

title: Song Of The Spring To The Summer

title: A Poet's Wife
content:

title: I Am The Way
content:
Thou art the Way.
    Hadst Thou been nothing but the goal,
         I cannot say
    If Thou hadst ever met my soul.

         I cannot

title: Song Of The Night At Daybreak
content:
All my stars forsake me,
    And the dawn-winds shake me.
    Where shall I betake me?

    Whither shall I run
    Till the set of sun,

title: At Night
content:

title: Chimes
content:

    From the shaken tower,
    A flock of bells take flight,
    And go with the hour.

title: Meditation
content:

title: Sonnet--To A Daisy
content:

         Like all created things, secrets from me,
         And stand a barrier to eternity.

title: Sonnet--Spring On The Alban Hills

content:

title: The Shepherdess
content:

title: The Lady Poverty
content:

title: Parted
content:

title: The Moon To The Sun
content:

title: In Early Spring
content:

content:

title: An Unmarked Festival
content:

title: Builders Of Ruins
content:

title: The Poet To His Childhood
content:

title: West Wind In Winter
content:

title: To The Beloved Dead--A Lament
content:
Beloved, thou art like a tune that idle fingers
         Play on a window-pane.
    The time is there, the form of music lingers;

title: Song
content:
As the inhastening tide doth roll,
    Dear and desired, along the whole
         Wide shining strand, and floods the caves,

title: After A Parting
Veni Creator
content:
So humble things Thou hast borne for us, O God,
    Left'st Thou a path of lowliness untrod?
    Yes, one, till now; another Olive-Garden.

title: November Blue
content:
